steps.title: Isosceles right triangle relationships
steps.content:
In an isosceles right triangle with equal legs of length $a$, the hypotenuse is $a\sqrt{2}$ by the Pythagorean theorem. The perimeter is:
$$a + a + a\sqrt{2} = a(2 + \sqrt{2})$$

steps.title: Backsolving with choice C
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C (18): If the hypotenuse is 18, then since the hypotenuse equals $a\sqrt{2}$, we have $a = \frac{18}{\sqrt{2}} = 9\sqrt{2}$. The perimeter is:
$$2(9\sqrt{2}) + 18 = 18\sqrt{2} + 18 = 18 + 18\sqrt{2}$$
This matches the given perimeter exactly. That matches choice C — C is correct.
